heading: Presidential actions in response to particularly severe violations of religious freedom
label: 6442 Presidential actions in response to particularly severe violations of religious freedom

Amendments2016—Subsec. (b)(1)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than September 1 of each year, the President shall review the status of religious freedom in each foreign country to determine whether the government of that country has engaged in or tolerated particularly severe violations of religious freedom in that country during the preceding 12 months or since the date of the last review of that country under this subparagraph, whichever period is longer. The President shall designate each country the government of which has engaged in or tolerated violations described in this subparagraph as a country of particular concern for religious freedom.”Pub. children.heading: Response to particularly severe violations of religious freedom
children.label: a Response to particularly severe violations of religious freedom
A
children.text: to oppose particularly severe violations of religious freedom that are or have been engaged in or tolerated by the governments of foreign countries; and

children.label: B
children.text: to promote the right to freedom of religion in those countries through the actions described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Requirement of Presidential action
children.label: 2 Requirement of Presidential action
children.text: Whenever the President determines that the government of a foreign country has engaged in or tolerated particularly severe violations of religious freedom, the President shall oppose such violations and promote the right to religious freedom through one or more of the actions described in subsection (c).

children.heading: Designations of countries of particular concern for religious freedom
children.label: b Designations of countries of particular concern for religious freedom
i
children.text: review the status of religious freedom in each foreign country to determine whether the government of that country has engaged in or tolerated particularly severe violations of religious freedom in each such country during the preceding 12 months or longer;

children.label: ii
children.text: designate each country the government of which has engaged in or tolerated violations described in clause (i) as a country of particular concern for religious freedom; and

children.label: iii
children.text: designate each country that engaged in or tolerated severe violations of religious freedom during the previous year, but does not meet, in the opinion of the President at the time of publication of the Annual Report, all of the criteria described in for designation under clause (ii) as being placed on a “Special Watch List”.section 6402(15) of this title

children.heading: Basis of review
children.label: B Basis of review
children.text: Each review conducted under subparagraph (A) shall be based upon information contained in the latest Country Reports on Human Rights Practices, the Annual Report, and on any other evidence available and shall take into account any findings or recommendations by the Commission with respect to the foreign country.

children.heading: Implementation
children.label: C Implementation
children.text: Any review under subparagraph (A) of a foreign country may take place singly or jointly with the review of one or more countries and may take place at any time before the date on which each Annual Report is submitted under .section 6412(b) of this title

children.heading: Determinations of responsible parties
children.label: 2 Determinations of responsible parties
children.text: For the government of each country designated as a country of particular concern for religious freedom under paragraph (1)(A), the President shall seek to determine the agency or instrumentality thereof and the specific officials thereof that are responsible for the particularly severe violations of religious freedom engaged in or tolerated by that government in order to appropriately target Presidential actions under this section in response.

children.label: i
children.text: the designation of the country, signed by the President;

children.label: ii
children.text: the identification, if any, of responsible parties determined under paragraph (2); and

children.label: iii
children.text: a description of the actions taken under subsection (c), the purposes of the actions taken, and the effectiveness of the actions taken.

children.heading: Removal of designation
children.label: B Removal of designation
children.text: A country that is designated as a country of particular concern for religious freedom under paragraph (1)(A)(ii) shall retain such designation until the President determines and reports to the appropriate congressional committees that the country should no longer be so designated.

children.heading: Effect on designation as country of particular concern
children.label: 4 Effect on designation as country of particular concern
children.text: The presence or absence of a country from the Special Watch List in any given year shall not preclude the designation of such country as a country of particular concern for religious freedom under paragraph (1)(A)(ii) in any such year.

children.heading: Presidential actions with respect to countries of particular concern for religious freedom
children.label: c Presidential actions with respect to countries of particular concern for religious freedom

children.heading: Presidential actions
children.label: A Presidential actions
children.text: One or more of the Presidential actions described in paragraphs (9) through (15) of , as determined by the President.section 6445(a) of this title

children.heading: Commensurate actions
children.label: B Commensurate actions
children.text: Commensurate action in substitution to any action described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In lieu of carrying out action under paragraph (1), the President may conclude a binding agreement with the respective foreign government as described in . The existence of a binding agreement under this paragraph with a foreign government may be considered by the President prior to making any determination or taking any action under this subchapter.section 6445(c) of this title

children.heading: Statutory construction
children.label: B Statutory construction
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph may be construed to authorize the entry of the United States into an agreement covering matters outside the scope of violations of religious freedom.

children.heading: Authority for delay of Presidential actions
children.label: 3 Authority for delay of Presidential actions
A
children.text: for a continuation of negotiations that have been commenced with the government of that country to bring about a cessation of the violations by the foreign country;

children.label: B
children.text: for a continuation of multilateral negotiations into which the United States has entered to bring about a cessation of the violations by the foreign country;

children.label: i
children.text: for a review of corrective action taken by the foreign country after designation of such country as a country of particular concern; or

children.label: ii
children.text: in anticipation that corrective action will be taken by the foreign country during the 90-day period,

children.heading: Exception for ongoing Presidential action under this chapter
children.label: 4 Exception for ongoing Presidential action under this chapter
A
children.text: the President has taken action pursuant to this chapter in a preceding year;

children.label: B
children.text: such action is in effect at the time the country is designated as a country of particular concern for religious freedom under this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: the President reports to Congress the information described in section 6444(a)(1), (2), (3), and (4) of this title regarding the actions in effect with respect to the country.

children.heading: Exception for ongoing, multiple, broad-based sanctions in response to human rights violations
children.label: 5 Exception for ongoing, multiple, broad-based sanctions in response to human rights violations
children.text: At the time the President determines a country to be a country of particular concern, if that country is already subject to multiple, broad-based sanctions imposed in significant part in response to human rights abuses, and such sanctions are ongoing, the President may determine that one or more of these sanctions also satisfies the requirements of this subsection. In a report to Congress pursuant to section 6444(a)(1), (2), (3), and (4) of this title, and, as applicable, to , the President shall designate the specific sanction or sanctions that the President determines satisfy the requirements under this subsection and include a description of the impact of such sanction or sanctions on each country. The sanctions so designated shall remain in effect subject to .section 6448 of this titlesection 6449 of this title

children.heading: Statutory construction
children.label: d Statutory construction
children.text: A determination under this chapter, or any amendment made by this chapter, that a foreign country has engaged in or tolerated particularly severe violations of religious freedom shall not be construed to require the termination of assistance or other activities with respect to that country under any other provision of law, including section 2151n or 2304 of this title.
